授業目的: この授業の目的は、アメリカの各地域ごとのさまざまな文化を概観し、その特徴や現代的な問題についてのディスカッションを通じて、アメリカ文化に関する知見を深めることである。
到達目標: ・"Cultures in the USA"に関するキーワードを理解し、使えるようになること。（例：Puritanism, self-reliance, freedom, diversity, multiculturalism, frontier spirit）・教科書および関連動画の精緻な読解を通じて、作り手によって捉えられた「アメリカ文化」を読み取り、他の「アメリカ観」と照らし合わせることができるようになること。・他者との協働により、「アメリカ文化」をめぐる現代的な諸問題について探究すること。
授業の概要・背景: As the course title “Cultures in the USA” makes clear, American culture is not monolithic but diverse. While surveying the characteristics of each region as expressed through both text and videos, students will explore America's history and culture through its diverse facets and explore its contemporary issues.
授業方法: This course is offered as a quarter-based course. In-person classes during Core Weeks will be held during periods 1 and 2, and will primarily consist of group discussions and presentations (in English and/or Japanese) by enrolled students.The instructor is not a native English speaker, but has studied English in Japan and has a total of approximately two years of continuous overseas experience. By sharing my own journey as a non-native English speaker navigating the language, I hope to offer insights that will help students use English as a global common language, express themselves, and communicate with others in our increasingly globalized world.

学位授与の方針との関連Relationship to Diploma Polic: Each course is positioned within the curriculum in accordance with the diploma policy andcurriculum policy established by each undergraduate/graduate school. By proceeding with the study of this course with an awareness of the DP and CP, the following abilities expected by each undergraduate/graduate school will be developed.North American Studies:1. Understanding and analytical skills regarding international issues: the ability to understand and analyze various regions of the world from different perspectives in the humanities and social sciences.2. Problem-solving skills: the ability to proactively identify problems and solve them based on appropriate methods.3. Intercultural understanding and multicultural coexistence: the ability to be sensitive to different cultures and to coexist with diverse cultures.
